note: Bug-fix + docs release. No scoring model changes. recall@10 metrics carried forward analytically from v0.6.2 real-DB bench (benchmarks/gate/v0.6.2.json). Carry-forward is valid: #variable_conflict is a PL/pgSQL compile-time directive that resolves name binding only — it does not alter query execution, index selection, ranking formula, or output values.

r1_fix.description: #variable_conflict use_column added to recall_lessons() and recall_hybrid() function bodies

carry_forward_rationale: v0.6.3 contains no changes to scoring formula, retrieval candidates, ranking logic, or index configuration. The #variable_conflict directive is resolved at PL/pgSQL compile time and has no effect on the query plan or result ordering.

tests: smoke_recall_hybrid.py::recall_lessons (v0.6.3 addition — R1 AmbiguousColumn regression guard)
smoke_gate.new_test: role_no_ambiguity — verifies role column value returned by recall_lessons() and recall_hybrid()
